tool.name: RepeatMasker
tool.description: screen DNA sequences for interspersed repeats and low complexity regions

param.label: Repeat library source
param.name: source_type
param.help: Use a Dfam library installed by your administrator, or supply your own library of repeats as FASTA (e.g. a RepBase export or a RepeatModeler consensus library).
param.option: Dfam library (installed via the RepeatMasker Dfam data manager)

validator.message: No Dfam library is available. An administrator must install one using the RepeatMasker Dfam library data manager.

param.name: species_name
param.label: Repeat source species
param.help: Source species or clade name used to select repeats from the Dfam library (e.g. 'Homo sapiens', 'Mammalia', 'Drosophila'). Families for this taxon together with its ancestors and descendants are used for masking.

param.label: Output annotation of repeats in GFF format
Ignore stretches of Ns when computing statistics
param.help: Scaffolds are sometimes joined with stretches of 25 or more Ns. This option ignores them when calculating repeat statistics

param.label: Perform softmasking instead of hardmasking
param.help: Output repetitive regions as lowercase, non-repetitive regions as uppercase (instead of replacing repetitive regions with 'N's)

param.label: Only clip E coli insertion elements
Clip IS elements before analysis
param.help: Normally RepeatMasker will report on IS element, with this option selected it will clip them before analysis

param.label: Only check for rodent specific repeats
param.help: If this option is select a check for rodent specific repeats is done instead of a full RepeatMasker run

param.label: Only check for primate specific repeats
param.help: If this option is select a check for primate specific repeats is done instead of a full RepeatMasker run

param.label: No low complexity masking
param.help: Skip masking of simple tandem repeats and low complexity regions.

param.label: No interspersed repeat masking
param.help: Only mask simple repeats, skip masking of interspersed repeats.

param.label: No repeat-like-RNA masking
param.help: Skip masking of small pol III transcribed RNA (these are masked by default because they resemble SINEs)

param.label: Limit masking to less diverged (younger) repeats
Maximum contiguous sequence searched
param.help: Maximum length of sequencing that is search without fragmenting

param.label: Select matrices for this GC%
param.help: Valid values are a percentage or -1 to choose the default

param.label: Calculate GC % for all sequences
param.help: By default RepeatMasker skips calculating GC % for small sequences

param.name: xout
param.label: Mask with X instead of N characters

param.name: invert_alignments
param.label: Invert alignments in alignment file
param.help: Show alignments in the orientation of the repeat sequence, not the query sequence

param.label: Output list of potentially polymorphic microsatellites

data.name: output_gff
data.label: RepeatMasker repeat annotation on ${on_string}
data.filter: not advanced['is_only'] and gff is True

tool.help:
RepeatMasker is a program that screens DNA for interspersed repeats and low complexity DNA sequences.
**Repeat library source**
- **Dfam library (cached)**: use a Dfam_ library that your Galaxy administrator has installed with the *RepeatMasker Dfam library data manager*. Enter a species or clade name (e.g. ``Homo sapiens``, ``Mammalia``); the families for that taxon together with its ancestors and descendants are extracted from the library with ``famdb.py`` and used to mask your sequence. RepeatMasker 4.2.4 no longer bundles a repeat library, so a cached Dfam library is required for species-based masking. - **Custom library of repeats**: supply your own repeats as a FASTA file, for example a RepeatModeler consensus library or a RepBase_ export (RepBaseRepeatMaskerEdition, unpacked to FASTA).
Further documentation is available on the RepeatMasker homepage_.
.. _Dfam: https://www.dfam.org/ .. _RepBase: http://www.girinst.org/repbase/ .. _homepage: http://www.repeatmasker.org/webrepeatmaskerhelp.html
